data.content:
insulin application,skin condition	Lipo-hipertrofia
Lipodistrofia
Lipodistrofia is a condition that occurs when there is a change in the skin tissue where insulin is applied frequently, often leading to lipo-hipertrofia.

chemical,hormone	Glicose
Hormônio
Glicose is a type of sugar that is essential for the body's energy production, which is regulated by hormones produced by the pâncreas.

condition,insulin production	Diabetes tipo 1
Diabetes tipo 2
Diabetes tipo 1 is a condition where the body is unable to produce insulin due to an autoimmune response, while diabetes tipo 2 is a condition where the body is resistant to insulin and is unable to use it effectively.

healthcare professional,method	Profissional de saúde
Rodízio
Rodízio is a method of rotating the injection sites to prevent lipodistrofia, which is recommended by a profissional de saúde.

organ,system	Células do pâncreas
Sistema de defesa
Sistema de defesa is the body's immune system that responds to threats and infections, which is compromised in diabetes tipo 1 due to the destruction of células do pâncreas.

equipment,medication	Agulha
Insulina
Insulina is a medication that is administered via an agulha, which is a needle used for injecting insulin into the body.

insulin application,skin condition	Lipo-hipertrofia
Lipoatrofia
Lipo-hipertrofia is a type of lipodistrofia where the skin becomes hardened and nodular due to the accumulation of fat in the area of insulin application, while lipoatrofia is a rare type where there is a loss of fat in the area.

condition,system	Disglicemia
Memória metabólica
Disglicemia is a condition where the blood glucose levels are not balanced, resulting in frequent fluctuations, which can lead to the development of memória metabólica.

organ	Células do pâncreas
Pâncreas
Pâncreas is an organ that produces hormones and enzymes necessary for digestion and energy production, which contains células do pâncreas that produce insulin.

blood glucose control,guidelines	Grupo de Pacientes Hemoglobina Glicada
Hemoglobina Glicada (HbA1c)
The Grupo de Pacientes Hemoglobina Glicada provides guidelines for blood glucose control based on Hemoglobin A1c levels.

blood glucose measurement,postprandial range	Glicemia após as Refeições (2h)
Glicemia em Jejum
Fasting blood glucose is a measure of blood glucose levels, and postprandial blood glucose is a measure of blood glucose levels 2 hours after a meal.

blood glucose range,hypoglycemia	Hipoglicemia (<70 mg/dL)
Tempo no Alvo (70–180 mg/dL)
The target range for blood glucose is between 70 and 180 mg/dL, and hypoglycemia is a blood glucose level below 70 mg/dL.

age-based guidelines,blood glucose targets	Adultos em geral (DM1 ou DM2)
Crianças e adolescentes (DM1)
Adults with diabetes type 1 or 2 are advised to follow specific blood glucose targets, and children and adolescents with diabetes type 1 are advised to follow specific blood glucose targets.

age-based guidelines,blood glucose targets	Idosos com doenças ou limitações
Idosos saudáveis
Healthy elderly individuals are advised to follow specific blood glucose targets, and elderly individuals with diseases or limitations are advised to follow specific blood glucose targets.

condition	Complicações
Disglicemia
Complicações are the long-term consequences of frequent fluctuations in blood glucose levels, which can be prevented by maintaining a balanced blood glucose level.

blood glucose measurement,fasting range	Glicemia em Jejum
Hemoglobina Glicada (HbA1c)
Hemoglobin A1c is a measure of average blood glucose control over the past 2-3 months, and fasting blood glucose is a measure of blood glucose levels after an overnight fast.

blood glucose targets,pregnancy-based guidelines	Diabetes gestacional
Mulheres grávidas com diabetes
Women with diabetes during pregnancy are advised to follow specific blood glucose targets, and diabetes gestacional refers to diabetes that develops during pregnancy.

data.tgt_id: Onde aplicar a insulina e como ela age
data.content:
application site,insulin action	Gordura
Onde aplicar a insulina e como ela age
Onde aplicar a insulina e como ela age discusses the correct application site for insulin, which is the gordura.

glucose monitoring,pregnancy-based guidelines	Mulheres grávidas com diabetes
Sensor de glicose (CGM)
CGM is a device that measures glucose levels continuously, and women with diabetes during pregnancy are advised to use it to monitor glucose levels.<SEP>Women with diabetes during pregnancy are advised to use a CGM to monitor glucose levels.

condition	Disglicemia
Inflamações internas
Inflamações internas are a type of complication that can arise from frequent fluctuations in blood glucose levels, which can lead to the development of diabetes-related complications.

insulin action,muscle absorption	Insulina
Músculo
Insulina is absorbed very quickly when applied to the músculo, but this can cause hypoglycemia.

glucose monitoring,pregnancy-based guidelines	Diabetes gestacional
Sensor de glicose (CGM)
Diabetes gestacional refers to diabetes that develops during pregnancy, and women with this condition are advised to use a CGM to monitor glucose levels.

condition,system	Disglicemia
Vasos sanguíneos
Vasos sanguíneos are the blood vessels that can be damaged by frequent fluctuations in blood glucose levels, leading to the development of vascular complications.

insulin action,vein absorption	Insulina
Vez
Insulina is absorbed very quickly when injected into the veia.

absorption,application site,insulin action,product natural object	Gordura
Insulina
Insulina is absorbed into the bloodstream through the gordura tissue, requiring proper injection technique.<SEP>Insulina is absorbed more quickly when applied to the gordura.

absorption time,insulin action	Insulina
Tempo de absorção
Insulina is absorbed more quickly when applied to the braços, coxas, and nádegas.

exercise,insulin action	Exercício
Insulina
Exercício can cause Insulina to act more quickly and cause hypoglycemia.

heat,insulin action	Calor
Insulina
Calor can cause Insulina to act more quickly and cause hypoglycemia.

fever,insulin action	Fevereira
Insulina
Fevereira can cause Insulina to act more quickly and cause hypoglycemia.

insulin action,warm bath	Banho quente
Insulina
Banho quente can cause Insulina to act more quickly and cause hypoglycemia.

person,product	Canetas recarregáveis de insulina
Elliott P. Joslin
Elliott P. Joslin helped train nurses to educate people with diabetes on proper insulin use, including the use of canetas recarregáveis de insulina.

person,product	Elliott P. Joslin
i-Port
Elliott P. Joslin helped train nurses to educate people with diabetes on proper insulin use, including the use of i-Port.

hot compress,insulin action	Compressa quente
Insulina
Compressa quente can cause Insulina to act more quickly and cause hypoglycemia.

education,patient awareness	Educação em diabetes
Paciente bem informado
Educação em diabetes is essential for a paciente bem informado to manage their diabetes effectively.
